A very good looking set of gauges done in the classic white face style that always seemed more elegant than a black face gauge.  The kit is very well thought out including most everything you could need to install it.  You get a calibrated fuel tank sending unit to install in your fuel tank for an accurate gage reading. The oil pressure and water temperature gauges both include 1/8" npt threaded sending units that fit most any American engines and can easily be used with thread adapters to fit other applications. The gauges install a lot easier than the old style gauges that used to use a metal bracket that had a couple of studs on the back of the gauge housing that you had to get to stay in place while trying to get it to hold the edge of the mounting hole, not very easy or secure. These gauges use a threaded body and pvc nut that makes it a breeze.  The GPS antenna is included for the speedometer and works well in most areas but as expected it did have issues in tunnels and parking garagesb where there is no GPS signal. The gauges is red led back lighting that keeps then bright enough to see well but not to bright where they light up the interior. A very nice modern set of classic looking gauges.

I liked the first set enough to now get this second set- but I think when it comes to reviewing them, one must factor the low price in to the whole picture. I feel that these are certainly nowhere near the quality of the gauges typically used in real race cars and high end customs, but they are just a small fraction of the price. For the price of a high end competition quality oil pressure gauge- I can get this whole set. Perfect for a budget build, boat, or dune buggy but I would never consider these for a quality restoration or a real race car other than a LeMons or hobby class basher.
For many, the GPS speedometer solves so many problems compared to working with vehicles which have transmission swaps and complicated electronic speed sensors... skip all that and just go GPS, and they're typically more accurate once in motion. I certainly don't think these will last as long as high end versions, but considering the replacement cost if one fails- it's still cheaper to replace these once in a while vs. the expense of high end ones.
In summary- I'd cringe if I saw these in an otherwise high-end build, but they are quite at home in budget builds, basher track cars, etc.
I've had no failures in either of the sets I've used, and the instructions aren't written for people who don't know what they're doing- but adequate enough for people who have experience working with instrumentation before. The backlighting is a little uneven, but if I get another set of these I will try the black version instead. This set went in a Triumph Spitfire that was gutted and wired just to be an autocross car.
I like the way these mount for custom dashes. Drill a hole, slide it in, and screw it! Its not the highest quality but the price is fair, they look really good, we will see how it all goes.....

 The MOTOR METER RACING PRO Series Gauge Set delivers reliable performance with a sleek, modern aesthetic. Featuring a 110mm GPS speedometer and tachometer alongside 52mm gauges for volt, fuel level, water temperature, and oil pressure, this set is designed to meet the needs of car, boat, and truck enthusiasts alike. Installation is straightforward, and the gauges feel well-built and responsive. While the price is reasonable for the quality provided, it's essential to ensure compatibility with your vehicle before purchasing. Overall, this gauge set enhances both functionality and aesthetics, making it a worthwhile investment for anyone looking to upgrade their instrument cluster.

Anyways, 4 months went by after we rebuilt the motor and installed everything else, I finally installed my new guagues.  They turned on and all seemed to work properly, until i turned on my AC, and then the fuel, oil, and water temp all failed to work properly.  They went from normal levels to empty, or no reading.  They are wired properly, I have no power problems and am getting proper voltage from my battery and alternator.  I would save your money and buy something that has been tested and from a company that responds to you when you ask for help.

Setting up gauges is generally pretty basic and easy. All the gauges work well including the fuel sending unit. However, the Tac set up is complete junk! holding the back button down for three seconds gets you into the set up. You need to know the Exact frequency output your engine is producing, then attempt to calculate what H setting you need. My signal wire is coming from the coil, and the Tac basically doesn’t work at all. The instructions are horrendous and don’t supply calculations for the H value.

Very disappointed after I drilled a hole in my dash and now I have to look for a new Tac that doesn’t match the other gauges. The entire reason I bought this set is so all my gauges would match. Super disappointing.

I've already have used a couple of this brands gauges in other vechicles and knew that they were a very good value. I decided to use this full set in a custom bezel on my old truck restoration. Couldn't be happier with the performance and how good these look. Finally there are options other than the cheap gauge sets at part stores and the overpriced high end gauges.

And installation is a breeze, but before you order, make sure you have the correct size hole saws for each gauge, because without the proper size hole saws, installation is actually not nearly as easy, you don't want to Dremel and file the holes for these, lol.  Overall, this is an excellent gauge set, for whatever you need them for.  The quality is fantastic and the price isn't so bad either.

This product is a fine looking set of gauges.  In fact they are a fantastic looking set of gauges and they looked absolutely AWSOME in my custom built dash that I built specifically for these gauges.  And that's where it all goes down hill.  The tac is jumpy with poor response.  I see others have mentioned that in their posts too.  The Temp gauge quit working in one day.  Also you will need to buy an adaptor for your temp sensor if you are installing in a GM product.  The adaptor in the kit will not fit GM.  Speaking of sensors.  Read them carefully.  Notice the Ohm range on these sensors are a little uncommon.  Meaning use the sensors provided, you stock ones will not work with these gauges.  Leading me to the fuel gauge,  With an in tank pump this is a bear.  They provide you with a cut in top load sending unit "Generic"  however this would require you to cut your fuel tank.  Folks if you don't know what your doing when cutting a fuel tank just don't do it.  That can be extremely dangerous.  Anyway I finally custom built a bracket connected to the existing fuel pump assembly.  The pick up on the sending unit was not adjusted correctly from the factory.  I had to bend the sending unit pick up rod so when the sending unit reached dead top the gauge registered full and when the sending unit hit bottom then it registered as empty.  This is on the sending unit itself, I'm not talking about adjusting the float rod.  The volt meter worked.  The oil pressure sensor provided is very tricky to wire into a system that requires the oil pressure sensor to send a signal to the fuel relay.  Once again generic or OEM sensors will not work with these gauges.  I am so sorry, I swear I'm not one of these trolls out here bashing stuff, in fact I rarely write a bad review.  In this case, It is what it is.  I cannot recommend these gauges even to worst enemy.

After a lot of fiddling they're working, mostly. I'm not sure if the problem is my lack of mechanical aptitude, experience in doing customization or the product instructions but it seemed like a lot of extra work to get everything working. The biggest issues were the fuel tank sending unit not having a proper ground because my fuel tank has rubber cushions that isolate it from the chassis. And the tach bouncing wildly.

 I had to drop the fuel tank after installing the sender to get a decent ground which could have been avoided with better instructions. I've added an inline resistor to the tach sensor wire and that seems to have quieted the bouncing now it just reads way high on WOT. Not sure whats causing that might change the coil to see if that fixes it.

All in all the gauges look awesome and except for the tach function properly. It just seemed like a lot of extra work to get to this point.

Review_Title: Nice set for older truck ! Easy to install
Review_Text: I've wanted this set for a long time !  It's in my 1972 Chevy .  Wish it was metal , but it's a good set .  Was pretty easy to install , everything was labeled correctly to make it easy .  Looks very nice when installed , doesn't look cheap .

Digital gauges and GPS speedometer at a bargain price

This gauge set is an excellent all digital option very similar to another manufacturer’s “revolution” line. Backlighting is good with a decent range from bright to dim. Unfortunately, there is not an easy way to tie the dimming feature into a factory style switch for all gauges to be dimmed/brightened at once. The color options are easy to change and uniform across the 6 gauges in my set. The wiring harness are clearly marked as are all accessory sensors. Longer wires would be nice for all gauges. The GPS speedometer is accurate to within 1 MPH of the analog gauge in my vehicle and the GPS readout on my cellphone. The Tachometer appears to only accept an electrical signal from the vehicles ECU. This interface is not compatible with carbureted engines or the ECU in my older Toyota vehicle. In order to use this gauge I will have to research an option to get a compatible signal to the gauge from the engine distributor. While the box is frustratingly small it protects all components well and the gauges are mounted into a piece of carboard that can serve as a ready made template for a custom dash insert. All in all the gauges look very good and with the color options will complement almost any custom application. Based on price alone I have to recommend these gauges for anyone looking to update the dash of their hotrod or weekend racer.

I haven’t set up all 6 gauges as I’m still working on my 98 eclipse project car but the 3 I have work as intended. The best setup I’ve seen is to place them in pillar gauge pods. Review_Title: Odometer gauge stuck at 99.9 Miles! Speedometer noisey. Looks awesome!
Review_Text:
I want to say that I really love these gauges. Purchased to upgrade my fully restored 1988 Chevy G20 Conversion van. Indeed, these took some work to make them fit in the stock gauge slots. I have to do this on weekends and when I have free time (not often) so it took several months to get to this project and finish. The gauges do look awesome... But that's where the good news ends.

After installing and driving around a bit, the odometer gauge got stuck at 99.9 miles. Also the speedometer itself is somewhat noisy and the needle bobs a bit at low speeds. Once you get going at about 35 mph it smooths out, but still has a bit of noise. For comparison, my stock gauges had no issue's like this.

Looks wise, these are absolutely beautiful gauges. But I'm very disappointed with the speedometer / odometer. Had I known, I would have gone with the GPS version instead of the analog version of the speedometer. In searching the Q & A - I see a few replies noting that none of the gauges can be independently ordered. :( I'd highly advise to avoid the analog speedometer.

A word to the wise: When I initially purchased, I asked the seller a question or two and he was awesome at helping. I needed to also purchase a few extra parts to smooth out the tach signal, and an adapter for my stock Chevy speedometer cable. However, because I waited so long to finish my project - I'm now out of warranty. I went through a lot to get this done, and I feel as if I may have wasted my time and money.

Seller, if there is anything you can do - please advise. I really do love the look of these gauges, as they have the vintage style I was after. I just wish I could get this speedometer to work the way it was intended.
They look great in the after market dash panel of the FJ40. Still working on the wiring, so hopefully I wire it right and the gauges read true.

Review_Title: Disappointed…looks nice at first, but too many issues
Review_Text:
I was excited about these vintage gauges because they look nice at first glance. But once installed, the idiosyncrasies became clear. One gauge’s faceplate is recessed more than the others, and the backlighting is uneven, each gauge lights up differently. Not the uniform look you’re hoping to achieve when buying a set.

The instructions were also inaccurate, especially for vintage car applications, and customer support has been lacking. Overall, I’m disappointed. With better quality control and support, this could be a great product.

Review_Title: The speedo is GPS not cable
Review_Text: It's for my 30 model a sedan. Just what I wanted

I recently purchased this set of classic/vintage gauges for my classic/vintage car. They look nice but that ends the good part.
In the directions for installation of the Speedometer they say to connect the green wire (which is the speed signal wire) to the ECU or engine computer unit.
NEWS FLASH!  Vintage cars don't have an ECU.
So I have sent 2 separate emails to their customer service address requesting a referral to an electronic sending unit which works with their gauges with zero response. I sent a third email to them and translated it to Chinese since they are located in Taiwan I thought that might help. No luck.
THIS COMPANY DOES NOT STAND BEHIND THEIR PRODUCTS.
BUYER BEWARE!!!
All Gauges are working except gas gauge and speedometer gauge 10 mph out of sequence searching for a problem.

Review_Title: Excellent value for this price point.
Review_Text:
Quick shipping, well packaged. First impression was very positive upon opening box. These are universal gauges so expect some minor adapting to your project.

Note: Clock in above picture NOT included in the kit.

Pros:  Simple and straight forward face, no logos.
  All gauges were fairly simple to install, work smoothly and seem accurate enough.
  Flat glass lenses.
Cons: On the Fuel and Volt gauges the light bulb/socket used is smaller than that used on the other gauges. The wires on these 2 gauge light sockets is very thin solid core aluminum wire that is a little on the brittle side. Usable but overall lighting of the dash at night is not consistent. Easy fix. Other gauges are fine.
  Instruction sheets are usable if you know what your doing anyway.  Not great otherwise.

Overall, good value for the money so far.
